arguments.task: Open and read the strongest official Notion pages needed to verify: product description, API authentication methods, whether access is self-serve or gated, API surface type and breadth, rate limits, and whether Notion documents an MCP server. Prefer canonical first-party docs pages that load fully. Return the exact page URLs visited and concise extracted evidence for each field. Also check official pricing or plan pages if needed to determine access tier.

input.thought: Start an official-source browser research pass on Notion developer and pricing docs.

raw_result.description: Workspace collaboration platform with a REST API for reading, creating, and updating content like pages, databases, comments, and users.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs describe Public integrations using OAuth 2.0, plus Internal integrations and Personal Access Tokens as bearer-style static tokens.
raw_result.gating_reason: Developers can self-serve create internal integrations, public integrations, and personal access tokens from Notion’s developer platform; separate Enterprise-only features like SCIM do not block building a basic toolkit.

api_surface.breadth: Well-documented REST API with endpoints/objects for blocks, pages, databases/data sources, comments, users, search, file uploads, views, custom emojis, and webhooks.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Notion officially documents a Notion MCP server for connecting MCP-compatible clients to workspace content.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official request limits page states an average rate limit of 3 requests per second per integration, with some bursts allowed; 429 responses include a Retry-After header. It also notes distinct workspace-level rate limits by plan.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs are extensive, current, and include auth setup, full API reference, rate limits, webhooks, file uploads, and MCP documentation; Marketplace listing requires review but core API use is self-serve.
